name: Muse Spark 1.1 (xhigh)
archDisplay: Proprietary multimodal reasoning model (undisclosed architecture), Meta Superintelligence Labs' first model family, update to April 2026 Muse Spark
note: First flagship from Meta Superintelligence Labs (led by Alexandr Wang), replacing the open Llama line with a proprietary model; base Muse Spark launched 2026-04-08, this is the 1.1 update from 2026-07-09, evaluated here at the 'xhigh' reasoning-effort tier. Meta has said it hopes to open-source future versions but this release is closed/API-only.

results.notes: Humanity's Last Exam, Artificial Analysis independent evaluation of Muse Spark 1.1
Agentic computer-use benchmark, 361-task split, xhigh reasoning effort; Figure 44 of Meta's official evaluation report
Sourced from Scale AI leaderboard per Meta's own report, mini-swe-agent harness
Terminal-Bench 2.1, pass@1 averaged over 5 attempts on 89 tasks, bash-tool-only agent harness
GDPval-AA v2 Elo, sourced from the Artificial Analysis GDPval-AA Leaderboard per Meta's own report
CyberGym pass@1, real-world cybersecurity vulnerability discovery/exploitation, from Table 1 of Meta's official Preparedness Assessment (helpful-only variant)

CharXiv Reasoning subset (1,000 chart-reasoning questions from the CharXiv validation set, matching this corpus's convention of using the reasoning subset for the 'charxiv' slug), run with a code-execution tool, graded by gpt-oss-120b as LLM-judge for semantic/mathematical equivalence; Figure 44 of Meta's official Muse Spark 1.1 Evaluation Report, Section 5.2.4.

GPQA Diamond, measured independently by Artificial Analysis as one of the 9 evaluations in their Intelligence Index v4.1 breakdown. Cross-checked by reading the same figure from two separate AA comparison-basket renders (Muse Spark 1.1's own page and Mistral Large 3's page, which both include Muse Spark 1.1 in their pinned comparison set) -- both show 90%.
LiveCodeBench, measured independently by Vals AI (±0.99 CI, ranked 18/127 models on their leaderboard). Not reported anywhere in Meta's own materials for Muse Spark 1.1.

MMLU-Pro, measured independently by Vals AI (±0.32 CI, ranked 11/120 models on their leaderboard). Meta has not published an MMLU or MMLU-Pro score for Muse Spark 1.1 in either the launch blog or the Evaluation Report.
MMMU, measured independently by Vals AI (±0.82 CI, ranked 11/82 models on their leaderboard).

SWE-bench (base/Verified-style variant, per this corpus's existing convention for the 'swe-bench' slug), measured independently by Vals AI (±1.72 CI, ranked 8/69 models). Distinct from the 'swe-bench-pro' slug already recorded for this model, which is Scale AI's separate, harder SWE-Bench Pro benchmark self-reported by Meta at 61.5.

verificationNotes: Base Muse Spark's own April 2026 release announcement (via Meta AI blog, relayed by buildfastwithai.com) reports Intelligence Index v4.0 52, HealthBench Hard 42.8, HLE 50.2%, Terminal-Bench 2.0 59.0, ARC-AGI-2 42.5 -- these are self-reported. Artificial Analysis independently measured Muse Spark 1.1 (xhigh) at Intelligence Index 51, a +8pt gain over the original that AA attributes mostly to Coding (+12) and HLE (+5, to 45%).
